title: Validate Operator Action Paths And Scope Behavior
description: Add regression coverage for the new TUI operator controls, including allowed and denied action paths, confirmation handling, scope-aware run behavior, continuous mode toggling, and non-fatal error reporting in the status panel.
acceptance_criteria: Tests cover one-shot run invocation and refreshed bead-state visibility after completion.
Tests cover retrying a blocked bead, including denied behavior when the selected bead is not retryable.
Tests cover merge confirmation alongside the new operator actions without regressing existing merge behavior.
Tests cover quick status updates for ready, blocked, and done, including confirmation and denied transitions.
Tests cover `--feature-root` scoping for run controls and global behavior when no scope is set.
Tests verify action failures do not crash the runtime and are surfaced in the status panel with action/result feedback.

handoff_summary.completed: Updated tests in tests/test_tui.py to cover: global one-shot scheduler runs refreshing visible bead state after completion, scheduler failure feedback in the status panel, successful quick status update to blocked, and coordination between pending merge confirmation and quick status actions. Validation passed with python -m unittest tests.test_tui and python -m unittest tests.test_tui tests.test_orchestrator.
handoff_summary.risks: `pytest` is not available in this environment, so validation used the repository's unittest suite instead. The new assertions depend on current status-panel wording and TUI action names.
handoff_summary.next_action: Review the added regression coverage against the B0044 operator-action implementation and merge if aligned.
handoff_summary.conflict_risks: Low. tests/test_tui.py now asserts specific operator status messages and action/result labels, so downstream changes to TUI copy or action naming may require synchronized test updates.

conflict_risks: Low. tests/test_tui.py now asserts specific operator status messages and action/result labels, so downstream changes to TUI copy or action naming may require synchronized test updates.

guardrails.template_text:
# Tester Guardrails

Primary responsibility: Add or update automated tests, run validation, and report defects or missing coverage.

Allowed actions:
- Write or update tests relevant to the assigned bead.
- Run targeted checks and summarize failures clearly.
- Make minimal test-enablement fixes only when strictly necessary to execute or stabilize the tests.

Disallowed actions:
- Implement feature logic beyond minimal test-enablement work.
- Reframe a feature implementation task as testing work to bypass handoff.
- Perform review signoff or broad documentation rewrites.

Expected outputs:
- Completed or blocked JSON describing test coverage, validation status, and follow-up needs.
- Precise defect or coverage notes when the bead cannot be completed within tester scope.
